My Map Campaigns My Plan Contact Newsletter News Mel Visits Promise School Following Fire to Support Pupils and Staff Monday, 30 March, 2026 Sir Mel Stride, MP for Central Devon, visited The Promise School in Okehampton following the recent fire to meet with school leaders and discuss support for pupils and families. During the visit, Mel met with CEO Rachel Shaw to hear how the school is managing temporary arrangements and planning for recovery. The visit follow the immediate contact the MP had with the school following the fire... pages.title: In Parliament | Mel Stride
pages.meta_description: Mel was elected as the first MP for the newly created constituency of Central Devon in 2010 - one of the most rural in England. He was re-elected in 2015, 2017 and 2019 with an increased share of the vote each time.
pages.text_excerpt: In Parliament | Mel Stride Skip to main content Main navigation News & Articles In Parliament Is Mel Stride my MP? My Map Campaigns My Plan Contact Newsletter In Parliament Tweet Mel was elected as the first MP for the newly created constituency of Central Devon in 2010 - one of the most rural in England. He was re-elected in 2015, 2017 and 2019 with an increased share of the vote each time. From 2012-14 Mel served as a PPS (ministerial aide) in the Business Department and then the Energy Department. He helped to significantly increase the number of young people embarking on apprenticeships and helped to steer a major energy bill through the House of Commons (which has since helped to reduce UK greenhouse gas emissions). In 2014 Mel was promoted to the Whips Office as a Government minister. In 2017 Mel was promoted to Financial Secretary to the Treasury and Paymaster General. He led legislation which cracked down on tax avoidance and evasion and helped to deliver the largest ever cash injection for the NHS. He also helped to reduce business rates for the majority of small high street traders and increased Government funding for affordable housing from £7billion to £9billion. Mel was appointed as Leader of the House of Commons and Lord President of the Council in 2019. During his brief tenure he highlighted the need to safeguard our democracy against the scourge of fake news and spoke out against the abuse of female MPs. In October 2019 Mel was elected as the Chair of the influential Treasury Select Committee . His nominations came from across the House of Commons (including from Labour and SNP MPs), reflecting his willingness to work with colleagues of all parties for the best interests of the country. During the coronavirus pandemic he has been at the forefront of scrutinising the Government’s economic policies and the support schemes to help both employers and employees. The Committee pressed the Chancellor successfully on issues such as the need for greater support for the self-employed and for workers returning from maternity or paternity pay. On 25th October 2022 Mel was appointed as Secretary of State for Work and Pensions by Rishi Sunak. ​ In Parliament Recent Speeches and Appearances Mel's Background Westminster Trips Mel Stride MP Footer About RSS Accessibility Cookies Privacy In Parliament Is Mel Stride my MP? pages.text_excerpt: Contact | Mel Stride MP Skip to main content Main navigation News & Articles In Parliament Is Mel Stride my MP? My Map Campaigns My Plan Contact Newsletter Contact Leave this field blank Westminster Office House of Commons, London SW1A 0AA 020 7219 7037 [email protected] On Facebook On Twitter The quickest way to contact Mel is by email ( [email protected] ). You can also complete the form below. Cases will be considered as soon as possible and taking account of the urgency of the matters raised. If you are writing to Mel as part of a campaign group where many people (sometimes hundreds) send him an identical or very similar letter or email, please check the 'campaign responses' tab on the left of this page. The time saved posting a single response online rather than sending out hundreds of letters or emails in reply is very significant and is spent addressing the considerable and often complex individual casework that Mel receives. First Name Last Name Postcode Address id Address 1 Email Mobile number Message I would like to be kept up-to-date by the Conservatives on future issues that matter to me: Yes There will be an easy way to opt out of future messages. headings: h2: RSS feeds and news syndication
pages.text_excerpt: About RSS | Mel Stride Skip to main content Main navigation News & Articles In Parliament Is Mel Stride my MP? My Map Campaigns My Plan Contact Newsletter About RSS RSS feeds and news syndication News feeds Events feeds You can read the latest news from this website using your news reader, or display them on your own website using RSS (Rich Site Summary) XML documents, wherever you see the orange RSS symbol. What is RSS? RSS is a defacto XML standard invented by Netscape for news distribution, now used by thousands of sites. RSS is an open format that uses a simple set of XML tags to define a news "channel" that allows any site to provide basic meta-information about your new content (headline, link, description). Whenever you use our RSS feeds publicly (where anyone but yourself would be reading them), you should provide proper attribution to this website. We cannot provide advice or technical support in the coding and integration of our RSS feeds into your site or software. Additionally we ask that you cache the RSS feed and do not query more than once in any hour. Mel Stride MP Footer About RSS Accessibility Cookies Privacy In Parliament Is Mel Stride my MP?
